Okay, welcome to the leadership portal 2.0. So why did I redo the leadership portal? Because I felt like I could make this a little bit more clear. I felt like I could present it better. And I felt like over the last 10 months that I've learned a lot and I wanted to make sure that I conveyed the information the way it should be. So that being said, I wanted to give an overview of why this is so important to consume. Because over the last 10 months since we released this portal, what I have noticed is the gyms who are performing the highest, the gyms who are bringing the stable infrastructure are those who go through this course. And so I know that operations for an entrepreneur can be a little bit dry and going through all the systems and processes that need to be that you guys do this, that I want to sell you on why. So why is this so important? Fail from the inside out. So what does that mean? That means that out of the four out of five businesses that will go out of business within under five years essentially, out of if you were to pick 100 of them, that 95 of them go out of business because of their internal infrastructure, not because of the market, not because of the economy, not because of competition. And so I want to convey why this is so important to you. It's because you have to get the inside right because the inside is what would be reflected externally to your customers. An effective leader is someone who can build lasting relationships and communities. So if you think about what is your goal as creating lasting relationships is what creates a lasting business. You are in the business of building and nurturing relationships. You guys have heard Alex say it and I'm going to say it. You are in the relationship business that starts with your culture, that starts with having processes in place around having an actual culture. So what do you need to believe in order to succeed right now? Because the beliefs that got you here to where you are currently watching this portal are not going to take you to the next level. And so a lot of people are like, are you asking me to be someone I'm not? Yeah, I'm freaking asking you to be someone you're not. You need new beliefs in order to become a new person in order to have a better business. What are the beliefs that you need to adopt? One, you cannot boost Trump's strategy. And three, the best way to lose control is to try and control everything. Okay. Starting with number one, you cannot lead what you do not live. Why is this so important when it comes to operations? Right? Okay. The culture and the current state. Okay. A business simply is going to amplify what does that think of the departments below me? I think of them as ex-er not. The problems I have in my head and as a person will be amplified in the business. Right? Which is why it's so important that you lead what you live. Right? It's also not a coincidence that those who struggle in business usually have super messy emotional lives. Okay. So those who have marriage issues, issues with their kid, you know, undelt with childhood issues. Right? It's also not a coincidence that those that have a therapy done coaching, whatever to get past stuff, have great social lives, have super healthy business. What you do outside of your business actually affects your business. Who are is what your business will become. Okay. So you guys have heard Alex talk a lot about attention, right? Grabbing all these attention points. So what I want you to realize is that you only have a finite amount of human attention available. Right? What does it look like when your attention is limited? It looks like this image. You know, you have a bunch of attention on your business. You've got a bunch of it on your $50,000 in debt. You've got a bunch of it on your failing marriage. You've got a bunch of it on the fact that you drink too much. You've got a bunch of it on your childhood and the fact that you won't forgive your parents. You've got a bunch of it on the fact that you're overweight yet running a gym. Do you think that that makes you a good leader? Do you think that you show up well for your business if you have these issues? No. Who you are outside of your business is more important than who you are inside of your business. Right? Which is why you will be the bottleneck of the growth of your business. So if you are the one who has limiting beliefs, you will stop bottlenecking the business if you are. Right? Whether drastically or not, we are all bottlenecking our business if we're not where we want to be. We right now are at 95 employees. I am bottlenecking us from getting to 200 because of the beliefs that I don't yet have or the skills I don't yet have. And I know that. That's why I'm always seeking to adapt and grow, adapt and grow. You know, unlearn things that don't serve me. How do you do this? You stop avoiding the issues. You stop avoiding the overweight, the childhood, the marriage. Right? You stop dismissing those things as just how your life is. That's bullshit. It's just how you've accepted your life to be. And you stop making excuses. Right? You face every, every, every, every uncomfortable situation and you handle it. Okay? Like an adult. So anything that is on your mind, you need to write it down and then you need to come up with an action plan because it's going to manifest in your life in other ways. If you aren't, okay? So you'll be able to force yourself to grow. Okay? You cannot force your business to grow. You grow by relief. You need to take back the attention from all these other areas, put it back into your pot and then that will relieve you and it'll give you the attention to family. There's not much to distract you. Okay? Second concept is that people trump strategy. Okay? You can have all the strategy in the world for your business, but it doesn't matter if people don't walk in your door and you have customers, right? Right? Okay? It's the same if you don't have a team. Okay? If you don't learn to translate your skills to others and grow them, your infrastructure is incredibly fragile because it's 100% dependent on you. So I want you to think about this. If you were to leave your business right now for two weeks, what would happen? Would it go down? Would it maintain? Or would it grow? Right? And so right now, I look at it like at least get it to a point where you can maintain it, then it's okay. How do we focus on it growing when I'm out of it? Right? Unfortunately, what a lot of your businesses look like is this. Okay? It looks like you. You may have other people on your team, but are you actually utilizing them? Do you actually trust them? Do they actually have say over your business? Oh, that they are responsible for certain metrics. Right? The goal is to build people who build strategy. And that is the highest use of your time. Important things. That's bullshit. Because this is what's harder to do. It takes long to do. Okay? How do you do this? You do this by delegating authority rather than tasks. So tasks is like, here's this checklist. I need you to do all these things every day. Okay? Cool. Anyone can do that. Right? A computer can do that. In fact, a computer should do that. But the only way that you actually expand your org chart without feeling an immense amount of pressure, the only way we expand your business more, whatever, right? Is by delegating authority and saying, okay, I'm going to duplicate my decision making, teach these people how I make decisions. Right? That's what you need to do. Hey, this client wants to do this. This client wants to cancel. This client was mad because of this class thing. Okay? That's when you get bugged all the time. You're constantly distracted. Right? People are always blowing up with questions, questions, questions. Okay? That's because you're creating followers. You're not creating leaders. In order to create leaders, you need to give authority. Okay? Which means, hence, bring us to the third point that the best way to lose control is to try and control everything because they will literally break you. There's absolutely no way that I can control everything in our business right now. I've been the 15, 20, 25, 30, 50, 70, 100. Okay? And I'm telling you that I cannot control it. And so I'm like, if somebody can do it 70% as good as me, then like whatever, let them do it and we'll coach them and we'll work on it and start thinking. And so instead, you must guard your values but surrender your control. So what does that mean? That means guarding the values around if someone's making decisions, you must understand how do you do that? You repeat these two words as often and as much as possible, especially when you're new to this. You decide. Okay? So that means that you're pushing down decision making. I so often get people coming in with questions and they're like, Hey, Leila, what do you think of this? I'm like, I don't know. What do you think of that? Right? Because 99% of the time, if I'm guarding my values and this person who I hired embodies the values that I look for, then they'll make the same decision as me. Right? But I'm teaching them to rely on me if I always give them the answer. So instead, you come back with a question. I'm going to let you decide. What do you think? You decide. You can make this decision. I trust your judgment. Okay? And you have to give it before you get it from them. You'll never know unless you actually give it. Right? And so this leads to you making the decision that only you can make. Right? And I have to just tell you guys this. They're going to, were they thinking? Right? And that's because we're all human and you cannot be this hard on others. If you're not this hard on yourself, which means like, have you ever royally fucked up in your business? Right? Okay. Well, then expected that other as well. Okay. And the less you fuck up, the less they'll fuck up. But the reality is, sometimes people see grace from the outside and they think that you're being soft with people until they ask for it themselves and they need it. Then they think you're too hard. Okay? I'm having problems. If you're not experiencing discomfort in growing your team, then you're not growing your team, which is the reality of business, especially multiple businesses will be like, yeah, that's normal. It is normal. People problems are normal. I think issues with it and not knowing things. It's all normal. So just, but if you want to grow, you must be willing to experience the pain. Okay? Because those are the, because a lot of the times what people expect is that they're going to have all the success. They're like, yeah, I mean, if I'm all stuff is going to go so well, I'm like, no, what's going to happen is you have like micro failure, micro failure, micro failure, micro failure, success. That's what it looks like in reality. You're making progress because you're actually taking action and executing. Right? And if you're not experiencing discomfort and pain, you're not getting closer to your goal. So that being said, I hope you understand that this is impaired and let me also apply not just to the business you have today, but it will apply to any business you have in your entire life. And I say that being someone who helps out friends and has seen other businesses use these exact concepts to run. And you can use these same concepts, whether you're running a gym that does 300,000, 2,000,000, 10,000,000, 50,000,000. I'm telling you, these concepts apply. It is always simple when it comes to how to run a business. It is just never easy. So let's dive in.
